body: ## Purpose Closes #437 Note the terminology and design choices are motivated by Section 4.5 of [v3] https://arxiv.org/pdf/2209.11371 ## Content - Add prior into `Inversion(prior)` and `TransformInversion(prior)` - Add the corresponding extension of the state to update the loss function with the prior component - Automatically add `multiplicative_inflation` with default size `1e-3*min(1, 1/Delta t)` by default for EKI, and 0.0 for ETKI, if `get_impose_prior(process)` is true - users may override this at the `update_ensemble!()` stage, or with the `default_multiplicative_inflation` keyword when creating the process. - Added a new experiment `loss_minimization_finite_vs_infinite_ekp.jl` (see results below) in particular i also compute contours of the actual posterior density to overlay convergence - Added unit tests for the getters, scalability of ETKI, and ability to converge toward posterior mean without starting at the prior ## Misc - Add issue to create some docs notes about the implementation in the EKI and ETKI page. (issue #450) ## Results: (Mar 4, 25) **ALL the following are plotted with a deterministic "G" on the left, stochastic "G" on the right. The infinite time ensembles are initialized outside the prior.** finite-time inversion stopped at "T=1" infinite-time inversion until "T=100" finite-time transform-inversion stopped at "T=1" infinite-time transform-inversion until "T=100" sampler for longer times and plots of error in ensemble mean to the true parameter over time (ensemble std in dashed line) ---- - [x] I have read and checked the items on the review checklist.
title: Impose prior option (a.k.a finite-time vs infinite-time EKI)
